bug_fix_report.reproduction: Replace the fixed 200 ms sleep in test_export_ready_notification with a condition-based wait on the observable completion event; this deterministically reproduces the missing wake-up whenever the worker registers after the queue transition.
bug_fix_report.root_cause: Listener registration can occur after the one-shot completion transition, losing the wake-up.
confirming_evidence: All captured failures checked 8–47 ms before worker completion, while passing runs happened to complete within 200 ms.
With the listener-ordering fix, the stabilized reproduction passed 500 consecutive runs.
With only the ordering fix removed, the stabilized test failed within 12 runs with the same missing wake-up.
bug_fix_report.fix_boundary: Register the listener before the transition, leave production behavior otherwise unchanged, and preserve the condition-based test wait while removing the fixed sleep.
bug_fix_report.regression_protection: Keep the condition-based test and hand it to pc-tdd; the stabilized test passed 500/500 runs with the fix and reproduced the same failure within 12 runs when the fix was removed.

debug_journal.hypothesis: The fixed 200 ms delay allows the test assertion to run before worker completion.
debug_journal.prediction: Failing runs check before worker completion.
debug_journal.experiment: Instrument event enqueue and worker completion timestamps without changing the wait.
debug_journal.result: All captured failures checked 8–47 ms before completion; passing runs happened to complete inside 200 ms.

debug_journal.hypothesis: A condition-based wait will stabilize the timing race and reveal whether the completion event is eventually emitted.
debug_journal.prediction: The race becomes deterministic and exposes whether the event is eventually emitted.
debug_journal.experiment: Replace the test's fixed sleep with a condition-based wait on the observable completion event, leaving production code unchanged.
debug_journal.result: The test deterministically reproduced a missing wake-up whenever the worker registered after the queue transition.

debug_journal.hypothesis: Registering the listener after the one-shot transition loses the completion wake-up.
debug_journal.prediction: Registering the listener before the transition will eliminate the stabilized failure, and removing only that change will restore it.
debug_journal.experiment: Change only the worker registration ordering so the listener exists before the transition, then run the stabilized test with and without that change.
debug_journal.result: With the fix, 500/500 stabilized runs passed; with only the fix removed, the same missing wake-up returned within 12 runs.
